private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
AstroForge, a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Matt Gialich and Jose Acain, has raised $53M to date. The valuation analysis relies on a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (ClearSpace, Hubble Network, Sift, and Aalyria), spanning $274M to $1.3B, alongside five public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median EV/Sales of 59.8x.

Two quantitative methods proved applicable. Stage-progression anchored to the $53M raised and applying a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier yields $106M. Peer-median valuation across the four private comparables—weighted toward ClearSpace at $400M, a direct Series A peer—implies $400M. Public-comp anchoring was not applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target company.

The triangulated analysis across these two methods supports a valuation range of $90M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $253M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage-relative positioning and the substantial variance in peer valuations, particularly the $1.3B outlier (Aalyria). Key uncertainties include AstroForge's revenue trajectory, capital intensity relative to peers, and whether near-term funding events will reset the baseline. The $106M-$400M band represents the most defensible zone given current data.
